description: A double-fetch (TOCTOU) vulnerability in Generate_PushBoundArguments() in src/builtins/x64/builtins-x64.cc. The JSBoundFunction's bound arguments array and its length are fetched twice: once before the stack overflow check (to skip if empty) and again inside the push loop. Between the two fetches, an attacker can race to replace the bound arguments array with an empty one (length 0). On the second fetch, rbx becomes 0, so addq(rax, rbx) does not update the argument count register. However, the do-while-style loop still executes one iteration (the branch condition j(greater, &loop) is at the end), pushing one extra element onto the stack. This creates an argument count inconsistency: one argument is on the stack that is not accounted for in rax. When the callee later uses rax to clean up the stack, it pops one fewer argument than actually present, shifting the stack pointer down by one slot and causing the function to return to the saved frame pointer instead of the return address, potentially giving an attacker control over the program counter and enabling a V8 sandbox bypass.

fixes.description: Local patch generated from V8 commit 36dda0d20d17529bc239deb05fc3708793346d83 to make validation and patched-image builds independent of remote Gerrit fetches.
